title: Rivian Q1 2024 Earnings Summary
text: Rivian exceeded analyst expectations with $1.204 billion in revenue compared to the estimated $1.175 billion. This represented an 82% year-over-year increase. The company missed earnings estimates, reporting a loss of -$1.48 per share versus the expected -$1.15. Net losses for the full year are projected at $2.7 billion. Vehicle-level gross margins showed modest improvement, declining to a loss of $38,784 per unit from $43,372 in Q4 2023. However, these margins remain worse than earlier 2023 quarters. Full-year 2024 production guidance was reaffirmed at 57,000 units. Notably, management expects slight positive gross profit in Q4 following a plant optimization shutdown completed May 1. The company ended the quarter with approximately $7.858 billion in cash, down from $9.368 billion at year-end 2023, reflecting ongoing capital investments and operational costs. Rivian reduced 2024 capital expenditure guidance to $1.2 billion from $1.75 billion by shifting R2 production to its existing Illinois facility rather than building a new Georgia plant.
